div.p: x86-64 System V ABI — Line-by-line breakdown

g.text: Caller-saved: rax, rcx, rdx, rsi, rdi, r8-r11

div.p:
Green = Callee-saved registers
Yellow = Stack pointer
Blue = Instruction pointer

text.tspan: // rdi = from (save here), rsi = to (load from)
// rdi = from (save here), rsi = to (load from)

div.p:
Purpose: Save all callee-saved registers
Why: ABI requires these be preserved across calls
Cost: ~2 cycles each (store to L1 cache)

Purpose: Save current stack position
Critical: Without this, we can't restore the stack
Note: rsp currently points to return address

Purpose: Capture where to resume later
Trick: Return address was pushed by CALL
Result: When we "return" to this, we resume here

Purpose: Restore target coroutine's registers
Effect: We're now "wearing" the target's state
Cost: ~2 cycles each (load from L1 cache)

Purpose: Switch to target coroutine's stack
Critical: Old stack is now inaccessible
Invariant: rsp must be 16-byte aligned - 8

Purpose: Transfer control to target coroutine
Effect: Like a function return, but to arbitrary address
Result: We're now running the target coroutine

text.tspan: // When we jmp to target's rip:
// When we jmp to target's rip:
// rax, rcx, rdx, rsi, rdi, r8-r11

g.text: // rbx, rbp, r12, r13, r14, r15

// - Context switch is a synchronization point

g.text: mov [rdi+16], r13 // Actually saved to wrong slot

text.tspan: // WRONG: Stack not aligned for target
// If rsp % 16 != 8, first CALL will crash
// WRONG: Stack not aligned for target
